title: Metadata Parsing
rules: separate metadata recovery from semantic interpretation
treat attributes and flags as first-class data
avoid speculative reconstruction beyond available metadata

primary_when: the request is specifically about metadata tables, tokens, signatures, or attributes
the request is about enriching an assembly model with metadata details
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about plugin bootstrap, IMGUI, or CPU scheduling

solves: preserving low-level member identity needed for reliable assembly analysis
resolving signatures, attributes, flags, and references without semantic guesswork
supporting repeatable dumps and exact runtime target selection
does_not_solve: understanding full gameplay semantics by itself
recovering information absent from the assembly metadata

core_principles: Metadata is identity data first and interpretation support second.
Tokens, flags, attributes, and signatures should remain attached to the member they describe.
Lossless signature preservation matters for overload-heavy game code.
Attributes and compiler-generated markers can explain lifecycle and serialization behavior but should not be overread.
constraints: Do not collapse overloads by method name alone.
Do not discard custom attributes, access modifiers, or declaring type context.
Do not infer semantic ownership from token order alone.
Keep generic arguments and array/ref/out details visible where available.
Preserve enough data for later lookup even when formatted output is simplified.
decision_rules: Attach metadata-parsing whenever assembly-reading needs exact member matching.
Use metadata-parsing as primary only when the user explicitly asks about tokens, signatures, attributes, or metadata tables.
If a method will become a Harmony target, require exact signature and declaring type information.
If a field will be read by reflection, record visibility, declaring type, and fallback names.
If metadata cannot resolve a reference, surface the uncertainty to dump-pipeline or assembly-reading.

preferred_patterns: metadata records attached to raw assembly model nodes
attribute and flag extraction as first-class records
avoid_patterns: dropping private members because they are inconvenient
flattening nested types without declaring context
using metadata tokens as semantic meaning without corroboration

recipes.name: Prepare metadata for exact hook targeting
recipes.when: Use when a runtime integration layer needs to patch or call an exact overloaded member.
steps: Resolve the declaring type and member name.
Preserve parameter types, return type, generic arguments, and modifiers.
Attach visibility flags, attributes, and token identity where available.
Record overload alternatives and why the selected member is the intended target.
Emit unresolved or ambiguous references as review blockers for runtime integration.

recipes.name: Validate reflected member identity
recipes.when: Use when a probe depends on private fields, properties, or methods discovered in decompiled code.
steps: Record declaring type, member name, visibility, member kind, and expected value shape.
Capture alternate names only when they come from real version differences or decompiler evidence.
Preserve attribute and flag details that may explain serialization, networking, or lifecycle behavior.
Mark ambiguous or missing metadata as a probe confidence risk.
Hand the exact member identity to integration-surface-mapping before runtime implementation.

pitfalls: Selecting a method by name while ignoring overloads.
Dropping attributes that explain serialization, networking, or engine lifecycle behavior.
Losing nested type context in formatted output.
Treating unresolved references as absent behavior.
Allowing semantic interpretation to overwrite raw metadata facts.
review_checklist: Are attributes, flags, and visibility preserved?
Can a Harmony target or reflection member be re-identified exactly?
Is metadata attached to the raw model rather than stored as detached notes?

references.title: Reverse Integration Map
references.interpretation: Use this to decide which metadata details matter for runtime integration handoff.
references.why: It keeps metadata parsing connected to practical modding contracts.

references.title: Dump Pipeline
references.interpretation: Use when metadata must be emitted into repeatable dump output.
references.why: It defines how metadata should remain searchable and diff-friendly.

references.title: Integration Surface Mapping
references.interpretation: Use when exact metadata must become hook or probe selection evidence.
references.why: It defines the handoff from metadata identity to runtime contract classification.
